description: Also known as Redirecting to Shiva Deity Page....
name: Redirecting...

keyMyths.title: The Cosmic Dance of Shiva (Nataraja)
keyMyths.description: Shiva's dance, the Tandava, represents the creation, preservation, and destruction of the universe. The rhythm and energy of the dance maintain cosmic balance and propel the cyclical nature of existence.

keyMyths.title: The Churning of the Ocean of Milk (Samudra Manthana)
keyMyths.description: Devas and Asuras cooperate to churn the cosmic ocean, leading to the emergence of various treasures, including Amrita (the nectar of immortality) and poison (Halahala). Shiva consumes the poison to save the universe, turning his throat blue.

keyMyths.title: The Destruction of Daksha's Sacrifice (Daksha Yajna)
keyMyths.description: Sati, Shiva's consort, immolates herself after her father, Daksha, insults Shiva during a grand sacrifice. Shiva, enraged, sends his ganas (attendants) to destroy the sacrifice and behead Daksha, later restoring him with a goat's head.

keyMyths.title: The Descent of the Ganges (Ganga Avataran)
keyMyths.description: King Bhagiratha performs severe penance to bring the Ganges river down to Earth to purify his ancestors' ashes. Shiva agrees to catch the forceful descent of the river in his matted hair, mitigating its destructive power.

keyMyths.title: The Burning of Kama (Kama Dahana)
keyMyths.description: Kama, the god of love, attempts to disrupt Shiva's meditation at Parvati's behest. Angered, Shiva opens his third eye and burns Kama to ashes, symbolizing the triumph of asceticism over desire.

sources.name: The Shiva Purana
sources.description: One of the eighteen major Puranas, devoted primarily to Shiva and Parvati.
sources.title: The Shiva Purana

sources.name: The Bhagavata Purana
sources.description: A Purana dedicated to Vishnu, containing numerous stories involving Shiva.
sources.title: The Bhagavata Purana

sources.name: The Vayu Purana
sources.description: A Purana, attributed to Vayu, containing cosmological and genealogical information.
sources.title: The Vayu Purana

sources.name: The Ramayana (Valmiki)
sources.description: An ancient Indian epic poem which narrates the life of Rama and contains references to Shiva.
sources.title: The Ramayana (Valmiki)

sources.name: Kumārasambhava (Kalidasa)
sources.description: A classical Sanskrit epic poem by Kalidasa about the birth of Kartikeya (Skanda), son of Shiva and Parvati.
sources.title: Kumārasambhava (Kalidasa)
